item.title: Digraphs
item.description: Definition A digraph is a set with a binary relation (set of ordered pairs made from elements in the set) on it. It is simpler than a group.

item.title: Law
item.description: A law is a universally quantified equality of terms.

item.title: Operation
item.description: Operation An operation is a function which takes a predefined finite number of inputs and whos domain is a power of its codomain.

item.title: Disjoint Cycle Notation
item.description: How to Parse When we have a permutation we can write it compactly by writing all the cycles in how numbers are assigned to each other.

item.title: Lattices
item.description: Meet and Join The meet of two points (a,b) in a lattice is the largest point, x, such that x ⇐ a and x ⇐ b.

item.title: Partially Ordered Set
item.description: A set which is transitive, reflexive, and anti-symmetric.

item.title: Permutation Groups
item.description: A group is a set with a binary operation that is associative, identity (unless its the empty group, thanks Luna), inverses, closed.

item.title: Varieties
item.description: A variety, V is a class of Algebras which is defined to be the class of ALL algebras which satisfy a set of predefined laws.

item.title: Basic Algebraic Structures
item.description: Algebra (\mathcal{S},☿,...) An Algebra is a set with any number of operations with any arities Magma A Magma is a set with a binary operation.
